corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: We tried this method but found it too destructive, distorting the signal from the attention mechanism and reducing performance.

context_window.previous: In this approach, each attention distribution is di- vided by the sum of the previous, which effec- tively dampens repeated attention.
context_window.current: We tried this method but found it too destructive, distorting the signal from the attention mechanism and reducing performance.
context_window.next: We hypothesize that an early inter- vention method such as coverage is preferable to a post hoc method such as temporal attention – it is better to inform the attention mechanism to help it make better decisions, than to override its de- cisions altogether.

top_candidates.sentence: We use the CNN/Daily Mail dataset , which contains online news articles (781 tokens on average) paired with multi-sentence summaries (3.75 sentences or 56 tokens on average).

Both the dataset's published results use the anonymized version of the data, which has been pre-processed to replace each named entity, e.g., The United Nations, with its own unique identifier for the example pair, e.g., @entity5.
In this work we propose a novel architecture that augments the standard sequence-to-sequence attentional model in two orthogonal ways.
In this work we propose a novel architecture that augments the standard sequence-to-sequence attentional model in two orthogonal ways.
We apply our model to the CNN / Daily Mail summarization task, outperforming the current abstractive state-of-the-art by at least 2 ROUGE points.
We apply our model to the CNN / Daily Mail summarization task, outperforming the current abstractive state-of-the-art by at least 2 ROUGE points.
The final summary is composed from several fragments. chunks of text from the source document ensures baseline levels of grammaticality and accuracy.

On the other hand, sophisticated abilities that are crucial to high-quality summarization, such as paraphrasing, generalization, or the incorporation of real-world knowledge, are possible only in an abstractive framework (see Figure ).
In this paper we present an architecture that addresses these three issues in the context of multi-sentence summaries.
In this paper we present an architecture that addresses these three issues in the context of multi-sentence summaries.

Therefore we apply our model to the recently-introduced CNN/ Daily Mail dataset , which contains news articles (39 sentences on average) paired with multi-sentence summaries, and show that we outperform the stateof-the-art abstractive system by at least 2 ROUGE points.

Therefore we apply our model to the recently-introduced CNN/ Daily Mail dataset , which contains news articles (39 sentences on average) paired with multi-sentence summaries, and show that we outperform the stateof-the-art abstractive system by at least 2 ROUGE points.

Therefore we apply our model to the recently-introduced CNN/ Daily Mail dataset , which contains news articles (39 sentences on average) paired with multi-sentence summaries, and show that we outperform the stateof-the-art abstractive system by at least 2 ROUGE points.

Our hybrid pointer-generator network facilitates copying words from the source text via pointing , which improves accuracy and handling of OOV words, while retaining the ability to generate new words.
We propose a novel variant of the coverage vector from Neural Machine Translation, which we use to track and control coverage of the source document.
The dataset contains many sports stories whose summaries follow the X beat Y score on day tem-plate, which may explain why our model is most confidently abstractive on these examples.
In any case, encouraging the pointer-generator model to write more abstractively, while retaining the accuracy advantages of the pointer module, is an exciting direction for future work.
In any case, encouraging the pointer-generator model to write more abstractively, while retaining the accuracy advantages of the pointer module, is an exciting direction for future work.

Note that the pointer and the coverage mechanism introduce very few additional parameters to the network: for the models with vocabulary size 50k, the baseline model has 21,499,600 parameters, the pointer-generator adds 1153 extra parameters (w h * , w s , w x and b ptr in equation 8), and coverage adds 512 extra parameters (w c in equation 11).
In this work we presented a hybrid pointergenerator architecture with coverage, and showed that it reduces inaccuracies and repetition.
We applied our model to a new and challenging longtext dataset, and significantly outperformed the abstractive state-of-the-art result.
We applied our model to a new and challenging longtext dataset, and significantly outperformed the abstractive state-of-the-art result.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
